item.title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

item.title: Channel Surfer lets you watch YouTube like it’s old-school cable TV
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The web app lets you turn on YouTube and surf channels like you're watching cable TV in the '90s.

item.title: Why Rivian is holding the $45,000 base model R2 until ‘late 2027’
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Rivian spent the last two years promoting the R2 as a $45,000 SUV. But buyers won't be able to pay that price until late next year -- if at all.

item.title: Substack launches a built-in recording studio
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Substack creators can pre-record video conversations with up to two guests, then publish directly on the platform.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup location, and price.

item.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to reengage its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

item.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

item.title: Lucid Motors shows off robotaxi concept called ‘Lunar’
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The concept was a highlight of Lucid's investor day, in which the company detailed its ambitions to push deeper into autonomy while pumping out new, affordable EVs.

item.title: Humanoid robotics maker Sunday reaches $1.15B valuation to build household robots
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Sunday is on a quest to build a household humanoid robot called Memo that helps with tasks like laundry and clearing the table. The company emerged from stealth late last year and already has 1,000 people on its waitlist.

item.title: Webflow buys AI content-generation platform Vidoso to bolster its marketing suite
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Founded in 2024, Vidoso uses large language models to help organizations generate marketing collateral like images, presentations, video clips, blog posts, and social media content.

item.title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Bumble's new AI assistant Bee will move the dating app beyond the swipe by matching people based on compatibility and goals.

item.title: Law enforcement shuts down botnet made of tens of thousands of hacked routers
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: An international law enforcement operation shut down a service called SocksEscort, which allegedly helped cybercriminals all over the world launch ransomware and DDoS attacks, as well as distribute child sexual abuse material.

item.title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for violating her privacy and publicity rights.

item.title: Tesla becomes a utility in the UK, setting up showdown with Octopus Energy
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: The new Tesla Energy Ventures division was approved by Ofgem to sell electricity directly to customers.

item.title: Gumloop lands $50M from Benchmark to turn every employee into an AI agent builder
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: As companies race to adopt AI, Benchmark general partner Everett Randle believes the key to success lies in empowering every worker with AI superpowers, and Gumloop’s intuitive agent builder is an example of the kind of tool that will unlock that potential.

item.title: Alexa+ gets a new ‘adults only’ personality option that curses but won’t get into NSFW content
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new Sassy style can curse and roast you, but the fun ends there.

item.title: Wonderful raises $150M Series B at $2B valuation
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The funding round, led by Insight Partners, comes just four months after Wonderful raised a $100 million Series A.

item.title: Rivian R2 launch: Here’s what $57,990 gets you
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Two years after revealing what its affordable next-generation EV would look like, Rivian has shared all of the pricing, colors, and performance specs for its upcoming R2 SUV. And like so many new car releases, it’s complicated.

item.title: Uber, Wayve, and Nissan plan to launch a robotaxi service in Tokyo this year
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Nissan will integrate Wayve's self-driving software into the Leaf with the goal of making the vehicles available on the Uber app in Tokyo.

item.title: Disney+ is rolling out its TikTok-like ‘Verts’ short-form video feed
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Following the success of TikTok and Instagram Reels, Verts is designed to boost daily engagement and reach mobile-first viewers, all while increasing discovery across Disney+'s catalog.
